framework.FrameworkName: Anti-Money Laundering and Counter Financing of Terrorism (AML/CFT) Compliance Guidelines
framework.FrameworkDescription: This framework provides guidelines for reporting entities to understand their enhanced customer due diligence (CDD) obligations under the AML/CFT Act. It outlines the requirements for compliance with regulations related to customer verification and suspicious activity reporting.

audit.Title: Enhanced Customer Due Diligence (CDD) Policy Compliance Audit

audit_version.ExtractedInfo: {"2801": {"impact": "Non-compliance with customer identification verification procedures can lead to increased risk of financial crimes such as money laundering, potential regulatory fines, reputational damage, and loss of customer trust.", "comments": "Focus on the thoroughness of customer identification documentation review and the accuracy of verification methods.", "evidence": "", "re_audit": false, "criticality": "Major", "description": "Compliance officers and customer service representatives must implement procedures to collect and verify customer identification information using government-issued IDs and other reliable sources.", "accept_reject": "1", "how_to_verify": "1. Review a sample of customer identification documents for completeness and accuracy. 2. Verify the authenticity of government-issued IDs through appropriate channels. 3. Cross-check customer information with external databases for validation. 4. Conduct periodic audits of compliance records for consistency.", "re_audit_date": "", "review_status": "accept", "why_to_verify": "Verification of customer identification is critical to mitigate the risk of financial crimes, ensure regulatory compliance, and maintain the integrity of the institution.", "recommendation": ["Provide regular training sessions for compliance officers and customer service representatives on proper identification verification procedures.", "Implement automated verification tools to enhance the accuracy and efficiency of the process.", "Conduct regular reviews of the Enhanced CDD Policy and Customer Identification and Verification Procedures to ensure alignment with regulatory requirements."], "selected_risks": [], "what_to_verify": "1. Customer identification documentation collected. 2. Verification methods used for customer identification. 3. Compliance records related to customer identification verification.", "mitigation_date": "", "review_comments": "Reviewed", "severity_rating": 10, "underlying_cause": "Inadequate training, lack of oversight, or insufficient resources may contribute to lapses in customer identification verification procedures.", "compliance_status": "0", "details_of_finding": "Common findings may include incomplete or inconsistent customer identification documentation, lack of verification of government-issued IDs, and inadequate record-keeping of verification processes.", "responsible_for_plan": "Internal Audit", "selected_mitigations": [], "suggested_action_plan": ["Conduct a comprehensive review of current customer identification verification procedures within the institution within the next 30 days.", "Develop and implement a training program for compliance officers and customer service representatives on proper identification verification techniques within the next 60 days.", "Establish regular monitoring and reporting mechanisms to track the effectiveness of customer identification verification procedures on a monthly basis.", "Review and update the Enhanced CDD Policy and Customer Identification and Verification Procedures annually to ensure alignment with regulatory changes and best practices.", "Assign responsibility to a designated compliance officer to oversee the implementation and maintenance of customer identification verification procedures."]}}

{"2804": {"impact": "Non-compliance with reporting suspicious activities can lead to severe financial losses, regulatory penalties, and reputational damage for the organization. It may also result in operational disruptions and legal consequences.", "comments": "Focus on the timely identification and reporting of suspicious activities as per the regulatory requirements.", "evidence": "", "re_audit": false, "criticality": "Major", "description": "Compliance officers must establish a process for documenting and reporting suspicious activities to the relevant authorities within 24 hours of identification using a standardized reporting template.", "accept_reject": "1", "how_to_verify": "1. Review the process established for documenting suspicious activities. 2. Verify the use of a standardized reporting template. 3. Check if reports are submitted to relevant authorities within 24 hours of identification. 4. Assess the completeness and accuracy of the reported information.", "re_audit_date": "", "review_status": "accept", "why_to_verify": "Verifying the reporting of suspicious activities is critical to prevent money laundering and terrorist financing risks, ensuring compliance with regulatory obligations, and safeguarding the organization's reputation.", "recommendation": ["Conduct regular training sessions for compliance officers on identifying and reporting suspicious activities.", "Implement automated monitoring systems to enhance the detection of suspicious transactions.", "Periodically review and update the suspicious activity reporting process to align with regulatory changes."], "selected_risks": [], "what_to_verify": "1. Established process for documenting suspicious activities. 2. Usage of a standardized reporting template. 3. Timely submission of reports to relevant authorities.", "mitigation_date": "", "review_comments": "Reviewed", "severity_rating": 8, "underlying_cause": "Lack of awareness, inadequate training, or ineffective processes may contribute to the failure in reporting suspicious activities promptly.", "compliance_status": "0", "details_of_finding": "Common findings may include delays in reporting suspicious activities, incomplete documentation, or lack of adherence to the reporting timeline set by regulations.", "responsible_for_plan": "Internal Audit", "selected_mitigations": [], "suggested_action_plan": ["Conduct a gap analysis of the current suspicious activity reporting process.", "Enhance training programs for compliance officers on identifying and reporting suspicious activities.", "Implement regular reviews and audits of the reporting process to ensure timely and accurate reporting.", "Establish clear escalation procedures for handling high-risk suspicious activities.", "Engage with regulatory authorities to seek guidance on improving the reporting process."]}}

audit_findings.HowToVerify: 1. Review the process established for documenting suspicious activities. 2. Verify the use of a standardized reporting template. 3. Check if reports are submitted to relevant authorities within 24 hours of identification. 4. Assess the completeness and accuracy of the reported information.
audit_findings.Impact: Non-compliance with reporting suspicious activities can lead to severe financial losses, regulatory penalties, and reputational damage for the organization. It may also result in operational disruptions and legal consequences.
audit_findings.Recommendation: ['Conduct regular training sessions for compliance officers on identifying and reporting suspicious activities.', 'Implement automated monitoring systems to enhance the detection of suspicious transactions.', 'Periodically review and update the suspicious activity reporting process to align with regulatory changes.']
audit_findings.DetailsOfFinding: Common findings may include delays in reporting suspicious activities, incomplete documentation, or lack of adherence to the reporting timeline set by regulations.
audit_findings.Comments: Focus on the timely identification and reporting of suspicious activities as per the regulatory requirements.
audit_findings.UnderlyingCause: Lack of awareness, inadequate training, or ineffective processes may contribute to the failure in reporting suspicious activities promptly.
audit_findings.WhyToVerify: Verifying the reporting of suspicious activities is critical to prevent money laundering and terrorist financing risks, ensuring compliance with regulatory obligations, and safeguarding the organization's reputation.
audit_findings.WhatToVerify: 1. Established process for documenting suspicious activities. 2. Usage of a standardized reporting template. 3. Timely submission of reports to relevant authorities.
audit_findings.SuggestedActionPlan: ['Conduct a gap analysis of the current suspicious activity reporting process.', 'Enhance training programs for compliance officers on identifying and reporting suspicious activities.', 'Implement regular reviews and audits of the reporting process to ensure timely and accurate reporting.', 'Establish clear escalation procedures for handling high-risk suspicious activities.', 'Engage with regulatory authorities to seek guidance on improving the reporting process.']
audit_findings.Report: Audit Finding for 0. Common findings may include delays in reporting suspicious activities, incomplete documentation, or lack of adherence to the reporting timeline set by regulations.
